name: MF-094 induced mitophagy but did not significantly reduce mutant mtDNA load
summary: A 2026 USP30 inhibition study shows a key translation boundary: mitophagy induction did not solve a disease-relevant mutant-mtDNA endpoint in cybrids.
statement: In heteroplasmic mitochondrial disease cybrids, MF-094 increased mitochondrial ubiquitination and mitolysosome formation, but chronic treatment for 3 to 6 weeks produced only a small non-significant reduction in mutant mtDNA load.
population_or_model: 143B cybrid cells with 40% or 80% m.8993T>G mutant mtDNA
time_horizon: 24-hour mitophagy assays and 3- to 6-week chronic treatment assays
quantitative_note: The abstract reports chronic treatment evoked only a small 5% non-significant reduction in mutant mtDNA load.
caveats: This is an in vitro mitochondrial disease model, not normal aging.
Mitophagy induction did not translate into the intended mutant-mtDNA burden reduction.
The result constrains targeted-mitophagy optimism for damage-burden reduction.
